title: digitalis lutea subsp. lutea
intro: Digitalis lutea subsp. lutea, also known as Yellow Foxglove, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is a subspecies of Digitalis lutea, a plant that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include cardiotonic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Digitalis lutea subsp. lutea include its ability to improve heart function, reduce inflammation, and support the treatment of certain cardiovascular conditions. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or extract, which can be used to create herbal remedies for its medicinal properties.
